sources.title: Preclinical data supporting expansion into neuropsychiatric indications (64th ACNP Annual Meeting)

asset.name: ORX489
asset.one_liner: Third OX2R agonist extending the orexin franchise into neuropsychiatric disorders

biology.simple_explanation: ORX489 targets the same OX2R receptor but is designed for neuropsychiatric conditions. Orexin signaling modulates motivation, reward, mood, and stress responses beyond its role in sleep-wake regulation.
biology.neuro_psych_rationale: Orexin dysfunction has been linked to depression, anxiety, PTSD, and substance use disorders. Restoring appropriate orexin tone may address both the sleepiness/fatigue and cognitive/motivational symptoms in these conditions.

mechanism.how_it_works: Third-generation OX2R agonist with a profile optimized for neuropsychiatric indications
mechanism.partnership: Collaboration with Nxera Pharma - $3M milestone payment received Q1 2026 for achieving early development milestone

lead.name: Neuropsychiatric disorders
lead.rationale: Preclinical data presented at ACNP supports OX2R agonism in neuropsychiatric conditions

preclinical.key_findings: Preclinical data supports expansion of OX2R agonist approach into neuropsychiatric indications

catalysts.what_to_watch: IND filing and FIH study design
